lines.text: Alright everyone, let's lock in the controller pick for the new line.
On our side we're recommending the seventeen fifty-six L 83 E. Six week lead time if we order by Friday.
Got it. The eighty-three E gives us the redundancy slot we need?
Yes, and the Ethernet I P bandwidth is doubled compared to the L 75.
What about the remote skids — are we keeping fifty sixty-nine I O on those?
The fifty sixty-nine L 320 E R will handle the remote drops, same Ethernet I P backbone.
From a purchasing angle, six weeks is tight. Can we get a unit pre-staged?
I can ask the warehouse if we have one on the shelf, but no promises.
Okay let's also drop the legacy ControlNet gateway. Seventeen fifty-six C N 2 R is end of life.
Agreed. Pure Ethernet I P everywhere. I'll update the network drawing tomorrow.
Send me the revised B O M before Friday close so I can cut the P O.
Maria, can you confirm the L 83 E firmware version we're standardizing on?
Version thirty-six dot zero eleven is the latest stable. I'll send the release notes.
Quick question — does the eighty-three E support the new safety task profile?
Yes, integrated safety with the GuardLogix variant. We can spec that if you need.
Not for this line. Standard L 83 E is fine.
Lead time confirmed at six weeks then. Friday cutoff for the order.
Sara, please verify the I O count against P and I D revision C.
Will do. I'll have it back to you by tomorrow afternoon.
One more thing — we're including a one year warranty extension at no charge.
Appreciate that. Alright, I think we're aligned. Thanks everyone.
Thanks Maria, I'll be in touch on the P O.
Thanks all.
Thank you, talk soon.
